response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and embedded software developer with over 20 years of experience, but his background is almost entirely in web development, mobile applications, and developer tooling — none of which maps to the core competencies required for this role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator optimization, deep neural network workload analysis, and hardware-software co-design at the chip level. The candidate's closest relevant experience — low-latency async I/O work at Sociomantic and robotic arm firmware at Altom — is too far removed from AI accelerator architecture to bridge the gap. His recent AI exposure is limited to MCP tooling and agentic workflows, which is software-layer work, not hardware optimization.
strengths: 20+ years of professional software engineering experience across diverse domains, demonstrating strong technical longevity and adaptability
Low-latency and high-throughput systems experience at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/second) shows some performance engineering sensibility
Exposure to embedded and hardware-adjacent domains (robotic arms, NI LabWindows C/C++ components, wave field synthesis with multi-channel audio hardware)
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or AI accelerator hardware — the core technical requirements of the role
No background in deep learning training or inference optimization; AI involvement is limited to application-layer agentic tooling
Skill set is JavaScript/TypeScript-dominant web development, with no listed hardware description, performance simulation, or chip design tools
